prestazioni: Engine swap to a naturally aspirated 2.5L Triumph TR6 inline-six (engine code: 2500CC/PE104) with a custom billet aluminum intake manifold by Rimmer Bros, featuring triple Weber 45 DCOE carburetors and a custom 3-into-1 stainless steel exhaust header by Janspeed Engineering, paired with a MagnaFlow 2.5" straight-pipe exhaust system for optimized scavenging and a 15-20 HP gain over stock.

Full suspension overhaul with custom-valved Öhlins DFV road & track coilovers (specifically revalved for the Spitfire’s 83.5" wheelbase), paired with polybushings from SuperPro (part #SPF1319K) and a custom-fabricated front strut tower brace by Moss Motors to eliminate chassis flex under hard cornering.

Lightweight flywheel (8.5 lbs) from Fidanza (part #191121) mated to a Sachs Performance twin-disc clutch (part #3000 951 001) with a billet aluminum pressure plate, coupled with a Quaife ATB helical LSD (part #QDF7J) for improved power delivery and traction under acceleration.
estetica: Custom widebody conversion kit by RML Group, extending the Spitfire’s rear fenders by 2.5" per side to accommodate 15x8 ET0 BBS RS wheels with 205/50R15 Toyo R888R semi-slick tires, paired with a custom front splitter and rear diffuser by Kamei for aggressive aero balance.

Full custom interior retrim in Alcantara (color: Triumph Racing Green) by Trim Deluxe, featuring a MOMO Prototipo steering wheel (350mm, suede) on a custom quick-release hub, Recaro SRD bucket seats (part #SRD-901) with custom mounting brackets, and a full roll cage by Safety Devices (part #SD0175) with integrated harness bars for FIA compliance.

Custom LED taillight conversion by United Pacific (part #99012) with sequential turn signals, paired with Hella Rallye 1000 headlights (part #1A6 007 857-021) and a custom carbon fiber bonnet by Fibretech Composites, featuring a functional NACA duct for engine bay cooling.
